articles.title: Anthropic Added a New Security Measure to Get Back Into the Trump Administration’s Good Graces
articles.subtitle: The government has removed restrictions on Anthropic’s Fable 5 and Mythos 5 AI models—but there were strings attached.
articles.author: Hugo Lowell
articles.summary: The government has removed restrictions on Anthropic’s Fable 5 and Mythos 5 AI models—but there were strings attached.
body: The Trump administration lifted export controls on Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 AI model after the company agreed to extend an existing guardrail to prevent users from trying to access certain restricted capabilities, according to two people familiar with the matter.

The safeguard means any users trying to unlock those capabilities will be notified that their request is blocked and will have their query processed by the less-advanced Opus 4.8 AI model, the people say.

Before Anthropic cut off access to Fable 5, user requests related to sensitive cybersecurity and biology capabilities were supposed to be processed by Opus 4.8. The new safeguard, the people say, will extend this guardrail to requests related to a specific behavior identified in a paper by Amazon .

According to an analysis published by Katie Moussouris, founder and CEO of Luta Security, after reading the Amazon paper, users were able to get around a restriction on Fable 5 by asking the model to fix code, rather than identify security issues in it. While cybersecurity experts generally don’t find this behavior troubling, the administration learning about it led to the showdown with Anthropic and the imposition of export controls, which as a practical matter took the model offline.
The addition offers new detail to Commerce secretary Howard Lutnick’s letter announcing the removal of restrictions on Anthropic’s Fable 5 and Mythos 5 AI models.

“Among other things, Anthropic has agreed to proactively detect and address security risks posed by the models,” wrote Lutnick, who led the effort to bring the models back online. WIRED first obtained the letter and shared its details on Tuesday night.

The Commerce Department also ultimately cleared Fable 5 for release after researchers at its Center for AI Standards and Innovation decided the safeguards on the model were sufficiently robust for now, the people added.

Still, while Anthropic has resolved its impasse with the Commerce Department, defense secretary Pete Hegseth has told advisers there is no clear path to lift his February 28 order designating the company a supply chain risk, according to a person briefed on the matter.
So while some of Anthropic’s challenges with the administration are less pressing, they’re not entirely over.
Trump administration officials believe they got the US Supreme Court to give them what they wanted ahead of the midterms.

In a 6-3 decision on Tuesday, the court opened the door for the first time for political parties to coordinate messaging and spending with campaigns, paving the way for Republicans to capitalize on Trump’s juggernaut fundraising operation.

And in the short term, administration officials tell Inner Loop that the ruling in National Republican Senatorial Committee v. Federal Election Commission disproportionately benefits vulnerable Republican candidates in this midterm cycle.
keyPoints: The Trump administration lifted export controls on Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 AI model after the company agreed to extend an existing guardrail to prevent users from trying to access certain restricted capabilities, according to two people familiar with the matter.

The safeguard means any users trying to unlock those capabilities will be notified that their request is blocked and will have their query processed by the less-advanced Opus 4.8 AI model, the people say.

Before Anthropic cut off access to Fable 5, user requests related to sensitive cybersecurity and biology capabilities were supposed to be processed by Opus 4.8. The new safeguard, the people say, will extend this guardrail to requests related to a specific behavior identified in a paper by Amazon .
paragraphTranslations: 据两位知情人士透露，特朗普政府在Anthropic公司同意扩大现有的防护措施，防止用户尝试访问某些受限功能后，解除了对该公司Claude Fable 5人工智能模型的出口管制。
这些防护措施意味着任何试图解锁这些功能的用户的请求将被通知其请求被阻止，并将由不太先进的Opus 4.8人工智能模型处理他们的查询，这些人说。
在Anthropic切断对Fable 5的访问之前，与敏感的网络安全和生物学能力相关的用户请求应该由Opus 4.8处理。新的防护措施，这些人说，将把这种防护措施扩展到与亚马逊的一篇论文中识别的特定行为相关的请求。
根据Luta Security的创始人兼首席执行官Katie Moussouris发表的分析，在阅读了亚马逊的论文后，用户能够通过要求模型修复代码，而不是识别其中的安全隐患，从而绕过对Fable 5的限制。虽然网络安全专家通常不认为这种行为令人不安，但政府了解到这一点导致了与Anthropic的对峙和出口管制的实施，实际上使模型下线。
这一补充为商务部长Howard Lutnick宣布解除对Anthropic的Fable 5和Mythos 5人工智能模型限制的信件提供了新的细节。
“除其他事项外，Anthropic已同意主动检测和解决模型带来的安全风险，”领导努力使模型重新上线的Lutnick写道。WIRED首先获得了这封信，并在周二晚上分享了其细节。
商务部还在其人工智能标准和创新中心的研究人员决定对模型的防护措施目前足够强大后，最终批准了Fable 5的发布，这些人补充说。
尽管如此，虽然Anthropic已经解决了与商务部的僵局，但国防部长Pete Hegseth告诉顾问，没有明确的道路来撤销他2月28日将公司指定为供应链风险的命令，据一位了解此事的人说。
所以，尽管Anthropic与政府的一些挑战不再那么紧迫，但它们并没有完全结束。
特朗普政府官员相信，他们在中期选举前让美国最高法院给了他们想要的东西。
在周二的一项6-3决定中，法院首次为政党与竞选活动协调信息和支出打开了大门，为共和党利用特朗普的强大筹款行动铺平了道路。
短期内，政府官员告诉Inner Loop，国家共和党参议院委员会诉联邦选举委员会的裁决使这个中期选举周期中的脆弱共和党候选人受益过多。

paragraphs: The Trump administration lifted export controls on Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 AI model after the company agreed to extend an existing guardrail to prevent users from accessing certain restricted capabilities. Any users trying to unlock those capabilities will be notified that their request is blocked and will have their query processed by the less-advanced Opus 4.8 AI model. Before Anthropic cut off access to Fable 5, user requests related to sensitive cybersecurity and biology capabilities were supposed to be processed by Opus 4.8. The new safeguard extends this guardrail to requests related to a specific behavior identified in a paper by Amazon.
paragraphTranslations: 特朗普政府解除了对Anthropic公司克劳德法布尔5人工智能模型的出口限制，此前该公司同意扩展现有的防护措施，防止用户访问某些受限功能。任何试图解锁这些功能的尝试都将被通知请求被阻止，并将查询处理交给技术较不先进的Opus 4.8人工智能模型。在Anthropic切断对法布尔5的访问之前，与敏感网络安全和生物学能力相关的用户请求本应由Opus 4.8处理。新的防护措施将这一防护扩展到与亚马逊一篇论文中识别的特定行为相关的请求。

articles.title: New York City educators and industry leaders gathered at Google’s offices to shape the future of AI in classrooms.
articles.subtitle: Google, the New York Jobs CEO Council and Urban Assembly hosted an AI summit for 150 education and industry leaders.
articles.author: Google AI
articles.summary: Google, the New York Jobs CEO Council and Urban Assembly hosted an AI summit for 150 education and industry leaders.
body: Technology can be a useful tool in the classroom as educators ask a critical question: How do we ensure today’s students are ready for tomorrow’s careers? To support them, Google, the New York Jobs CEO Council and Urban Assembly hosted an AI summit for 150 education and industry leaders.

The goal: drive knowledge sharing between those hiring for the future and educators teaching it. Through hands-on sessions like aiEDU’s “Vibe Coding” and Google’s “ Meet LEA ,” educators explored how tools like Google AI mode and NotebookLM can spark curiosity and build AI literacy.

A major theme emerged: AI’s true impact is the problem-solving it enables. Industry leaders emphasized that as technology streamlines workflows, "human skills" — like adaptability, collaboration, and critical judgment — become essential.

While embracing these advancements, attendees agreed we must remain uncompromising on privacy and equitable access. The ultimate takeaway: to prepare a generation for the future, technological innovation must happen with schools, not around them.
NYC Public Schools' Chief of Student Pathways Jane Martínez Dowling and Google's Steven Butschi share industry insights during a fireside chat moderated by Urban Assembly CEO David Adams.
Google's Tequila Lamar sets the stage during opening remarks at the AI Summit 2026.
Let’s stay in touch. Get the latest news from Google in your inbox.
keyPoints: Technology can be a useful tool in the classroom as educators ask a critical question: How do we ensure today’s students are ready for tomorrow’s careers? To support them, Google, the New York Jobs CEO Council and Urban Assembly hosted an AI summit for 150 education and industry leaders.

The goal: drive knowledge sharing between those hiring for the future and educators teaching it. Through hands-on sessions like aiEDU’s “Vibe Coding” and Google’s “ Meet LEA ,” educators explored how tools like Google AI mode and NotebookLM can spark curiosity and build AI literacy.

A major theme emerged: AI’s true impact is the problem-solving it enables. Industry leaders emphasized that as technology streamlines workflows, "human skills" — like adaptability, collaboration, and critical judgment — become essential.
paragraphTranslations: 技术可以成为课堂上的有用工具，因为教育者提出了一个关键问题：我们如何确保今天的学生为明天的职业做好准备？为了支持他们，谷歌、纽约工作首席执行官委员会和城市联盟为150位教育和行业领袖举办了一场人工智能峰会。
目标是：促进那些为未来招聘的人和教授这些课程的教育者之间的知识共享。通过像aiEDU的“Vibe Coding”和谷歌的“Meet LEA”这样的实践课程，教育者探索了谷歌AI模式和NotebookLM等工具如何激发好奇心和建立人工智能素养。
一个主要主题浮现出来：人工智能真正的影响是它使问题解决成为可能。行业领袖强调，随着技术简化工作流程，“人类技能”——比如适应性、协作和批判性判断——变得至关重要。
在拥抱这些进步的同时，与会者一致认为，我们必须在隐私和公平获取方面毫不妥协。最终的收获是：为了准备一代人迎接未来，技术创新必须与学校一起发生，而不是围绕学校发生。
纽约市公立学校的“学生路径”负责人简·马丁内斯·道林和谷歌的史蒂文·布奇在城市联盟首席执行官大卫·亚当斯主持的炉边谈话中分享行业洞察。
谷歌的特基拉·拉马尔在2026年人工智能峰会的开幕致辞中为活动定下基调。
让我们保持联系。在您的收件箱中获取谷歌的最新新闻。

vocabulary.example: Many educators are using technology in the classroom.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 许多教育工作者在课堂上使用技术。

vocabulary.example: The conference attracted top industry leaders from around the world.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 会议吸引了来自世界各地的顶尖行业领袖。

vocabulary.example: The AI summit focused on the latest advancements in technology.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 人工智能峰会聚焦于技术的最新进展。

vocabulary.example: Adaptability is an important skill in today's fast-changing world.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 适应性是当今快速变化世界中的一项重要技能。

vocabulary.example: Collaboration between teams can lead to better results.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 团队之间的合作可以带来更好的结果。

vocabulary.example: Online privacy is a major concern for many internet users.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 在线隐私是许多互联网用户的主要关注点。

paragraphs: New York City educators and industry leaders met at Google’s offices to discuss the future of AI in classrooms. Google, the New York Jobs CEO Council, and Urban Assembly organized the AI summit for 150 leaders. The goal was to share knowledge between those who hire for the future and those who teach it. Educators tried tools like Google AI mode and NotebookLM to build AI literacy. The main theme was that AI helps problem-solving and human skills like adaptability and collaboration. Attendees agreed that privacy and equal access are important. The key takeaway was that technological innovation should happen with schools.
paragraphTranslations: 纽约市的教育工作者和行业领袖在谷歌办公室聚集，讨论人工智能在课堂的未来。谷歌、纽约工作首席执行官委员会和城市联盟为150位领导者举办了人工智能峰会。目标是分享那些为未来招聘和那些教授未来知识的人之间的知识。教育工作者尝试了谷歌人工智能模式和NotebookLM等工具，以建立人工智能素养。主要主题是人工智能有助于解决问题和人类技能，如适应性和协作。与会者一致认为，隐私和平等获取非常重要。关键的收获是，技术创新应该与学校一起发生。
